title: Iranian State Railways: Summary Income and Expense Account, 1953/54-1960/61 (World Bank, 1962)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION, not bulk download. Original document is a 1962 scanned World Bank report (data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1962_economic_development_program.pdf), Chapter 4 (Transport and Communications), Table 5, page 81 (printed page -77-). Every value was verified by rendering the exact page to a high-resolution PNG (pdftoppm -r 200) and reading the table directly off the image. IMPORTANT transcription note: the source typewriter used a PERIOD as a thousands separator in this table (not a decimal point) for the Gross Revenues and Working Expenses columns -- e.g. the printed '1.269' for 1953/54 Gross Revenues means 1,269 (million Rials), not 1.269. This was confirmed by an exact internal arithmetic check for every one of the 8 rows: Gross Revenues minus Working Expenses equals the printed Balance before Depreciation figure only under the thousands-separator reading (e.g. 1,269-828=441, matching the printed Balance of 441; 2,592-1,564=1,028, matching; all 8 rows check out exactly). Values in data.csv are plain integers (in million Rials) with the separator convention removed to avoid ambiguity. No value was interpolated, estimated, or fabricated.
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
notes: Source document: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1962_economic_development_program.pdf — Chapter 4 (Transport and Communications), Table 5, page 81 (printed page -77-). Iranian solar calendar years (e.g. 1953/54 = 21 March 1953-20 March 1954). Companion table to wb1962-railways-freight-passenger-traffic-1953-1961 (same institution, same page range, same years). Part of a systematic effort to extract Pahlavi-era (esp. 1941-1962) data from primary-source PDFs already in data/raw/.
